↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇳🇨🇳🇮🇷 Reuters: The UN Security Council is expected to vote on a resolution on the Strait of Hormuz, but diplomats say the draft has been significantly weakened after China opposed authorising the use of force. Earlier versions included language allowing…" ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Iran’s Permanent Representative to the United Nations Amir Saeid Iravani says Donald Trump recent threats against Iran constitute direct incitement to terrorism and clear evidence of intent to commit war crimes under international law. He warns that threats to target civilian infrastructure, including power plants and bridges, pose a direct danger to civilians and that deliberately targeting such infrastructure constitutes a war crime and state terrorism. He calls on the United Nations Security Council and the UN Secretary-General to take immediate action to condemn the threats and ensure accountability, warning that inaction will undermine international law and embolden further violations. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇺🇳 🇨🇳 🇮🇷 Reuters : The UN Security Council is expected to vote on a resolution on the Strait of Hormuz, but diplomats say the draft has been significantly weakened after China opposed authorising the use of force. Earlier versions included language allowing “all necessary means,” but the latest draft instead focuses on defensive coordination to ensure safe navigation. @wfwitness
⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 The IDF published footage showing airstrikes on Iranian air defense systems. IDF also reported that more than 130 Iranian air defense systems have been destroyed during the war. @wfwitness

Visioner ‼️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) have issued a warning urging civilians not to approach railway infrastructure across Iran today, as intensive airstrikes targeting these systems are expected to begin shortly. The destruction of Iran’s railway logistics network likely serves two primary objectives: 1 — To weaken Iran’s economy by targeting and disabling critical railway infrastructure. 2 — To eliminate Iran’s ability to rapidly redeploy forces via rail in the event of a potential ground incursion. Notably, just yesterday, the Israeli Air Force reportedly destroyed dozens of transport aircraft and helicopters at their bases, assets used by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and the Iranian Armed Forces. This signals a broader effort by Israeli/U.S. coalition forces to neutralize Iran’s capacity for rapid movement and force deployment. See the latest updates with us: @visionergeo tweet

Open Source Intel IDF Spokesperson: The IDF struck another petrochemical complex in Shiraz - one of the few remaining complexes for producing essential chemical components for explosives and materials for ballistic missiles in Iran The Air Force, guided by Military Intelligence, completed yesterday (Monday) a wide-ranging strike mission in several areas in Iran targeting key infrastructures of the Iranian terror regime. As part of the mission, the IDF struck another petrochemical plant in Shiraz used by the armed forces in Iran to produce nitric acid, a necessary substance for manufacturing explosives and other materials in ballistic missile development processes. This deepens the damage to the military capabilities of the regime, focusing on the production capabilities of weapons that relied on the components from the plant. This is one of the few remaining complexes for producing essential chemical components for explosives and materials for ballistic missiles in Iran, after the IDF struck the largest petrochemical plant in Iran as well as the petrochemical complex in Mahshahr. Simultaneously, the IDF struck a large site of the ballistic missile array in northwestern Iran. From this site, soldiers of the array launched dozens of missiles toward the territory of the State of Israel. The site was attacked while soldiers and commanders of the missile array were operating inside to advance and carry out terror activities against the State of Israel and other countries. tweet

Open Source Intel Saudi accounts are restricted from posting about strikes inside the country, so some shift to the comments and claim it came from Iraq or try to push community notes. The reality remains: two Iranian ballistic missiles struck the Jubail Industrial Complex. For context, Jubail spans over 1,000 square kilometers with more than 150 industrial facilities, accounting for roughly 7% to 12% of Saudi GDP and about 50% of total foreign direct investment. It processes around 500,000 to 700,000 barrels per day and hosts major steel, aluminum, fertilizer, and chemical plants, supported by large power stations. This footage is confirmed and not old or from Iraq. You may see screenshots circulating that claim to be from Reuters in 2024, but if you look further into them, they are FAKE. tweet

Cheng Li-wun, leader of Taiwan’s opposition Kuomintang, is visiting China (April 7–12) on a “peace” trip to ease tensions and boost dialogue with Beijing. She is expected to meet Xi Jinping. It’s the first such visit by a KMT leader in about a decade.

"Was that F-15E the beginning of a raid? They had a plan ready to go. This was a package — designed to go to an airfield near Isfahan and launch a raid to grab Iran's nuclear material." Scott Ritter believes it was a pre-planned special forces operation to seize Iran's enriched uranium near Isfahan that fell apart when the helicopters were hit. With no Plan B, Trump converted it into a "rescue mission." "You would have killed a lot of Americans on this raid." FULL EPISODE!! 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
